Jack Hero Review - Empires and Puzzles

Jack brings a violin and a pumpkin bomb to the battlefield. Is this 3-star Halloween hero a trick or a treat? We analyze his explosive potential, his sneaky buff-stealing passive, and whether he still holds up in the current 3-star meta.
Jack: The Pumpkin Minstrel Who Plays a Deadly Tune
Jack, The Pumpkin Minstrel: Explosive Pranks and Delayed Doom
Jack is a 3-star Rare Dark hero from the

event. Originally released in 2020, he brings a unique "delayed explosion" mechanic to the 3-star arena, a rarity usually reserved for more complex 5-star heroes. While he might look like he's just there to provide musical accompaniment for a spooky party, his violin plays a requiem for anyone who can't cleanse his pumpkins in time.

Jack’s design is a classic "Pumpkin Head" trope with a dash of elegance. Dressed in a sharp purple suit with a top hat, he plays a violin with spindly, vine-like fingers. The glowing jack-o'-lantern head gives him a mischievous rather than terrifying vibe, fitting for a 3-star hero who is as much about the prank (the delayed bomb) as the kill. It's a whimsical design that stands out against the usual grim warriors of the dark element.
When comparing Jack to the modern 3-star landscape, the age of his pumpkin is starting to show.
Significantly lower than modern hitters like
(559). He relies heavily on his skill's fixed damage to compensate.
These figures are quite fragile by today's standards. Modern 3-stars like
sits at 1031 HP. Jack is "squishy" and needs protection or emblems to survive long enough to cast.
Jack’s special skill is a two-part act:
He deals 200% damage to a single target. This is a decent "slap," but not a knockout punch on its own.
This is his signature move. The target gets a bomb that explodes after 3 turns, dealing 206 damage to the target
nearby enemies. Crucially, this bomb also explodes immediately if the target dies or the effect is cleansed (wait, no—cleansing
it safely, which is the main drawback).

The bomb is a status effect. If the enemy has a cleanser (like Tyrum or Helo), they can wipe the bomb away before it detonates, nullifying a huge chunk of Jack's damage potential. However, if you time it right—or kill the target with another sniper before the 3 turns are up—the bomb triggers instantly.
Fast. He charges in 8 tiles (without mana troops), or 7 tiles with a costume bonus or mana node (though Jack has no costume yet).
200% of 491 Attack ≈ 982 damage value (before defense mitigation).
206 fixed damage. This ignores defense buffs and stats, which is excellent against high-defense tanks.
On a single target, if the bomb blows, he deals the equivalent of a heavy sniper hit. If it hits 3 people, the total damage output is massive for a 3-star (206 * 3 = 618 extra damage).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family bonus provides a chance to spawn a spooky minion. While unreliable, a free meat shield is always welcome on a hero with low defense stats.
This is the hidden gem of Jack's kit.
A 65% chance to steal the newest buff is incredibly disruptive in 3-star tournaments where buffs like attack up (Brienne) or defense up (Gunnar) are king.
If there are no buffs to steal, he deals
. This transforms his "Sniper + Dot" skill into a legitimate AoE nuke. If you fire him at the start of a match before enemies buff up, he hits one target for 200% and
for 150%. That is devastating at Fast speed.
Helps mitigate his squishiness, giving him a better chance to survive a hit from an opposing sniper.
Upon release, Jack was a fun novelty. Players loved the "bomb" mechanic but found it tricky to time.
Stat-wise, he has been left in the dust. A 468 Power rating is low compared to the 530+ of modern 3-stars. However, his
(added in a later balance update) gave him a new lease on life. The potential to do 150% damage to all enemies at Fast speed is something very few 3-stars can match.
He is not a plug-and-play hero for every team due to his low stats. However, in
tournaments, his passive AoE or fixed damage bomb can still win games.
Jack currently does not have a costume. If he receives one in the future, it will be critical for updating his base stats to modern levels.
Jack needs to stay alive to let his bomb tick or to fire his passive. However, his damage relies on Attack stats for the initial hit and the passive AoE.
Maximizes the 200% snipe and the 150% AoE passive damage.
He remains extremely fragile and might die before firing.
Helps him survive splashes from enemy tanks or flanks.
His direct damage becomes negligible, relying entirely on the fixed-damage bomb.
. Jack is a 3-star hero; in this rarity, killing the enemy quickly is usually a better defense than trying to tank hits. You want that Passive AoE to hurt.
Between the snipe, the bomb, and the potential AoE passive, he can output damage numbers far above his weight class.
The bomb deals 206 damage regardless of the enemy's defense, making him a tank buster.
He either steals a crucial buff or punishes the enemy team for having none.
A single cleanse (Tyrum, Helo, etc.) completely removes the pumpkin bomb without it detonating.
He dies easily to slash attacks and special skills.
You can't control whether he steals a buff or does AoE damage (it depends on enemy state + 65% chance), making him unpredictable.
His bomb goes off faster relative to the match pace, and his passive AoE is devastating when firing repeatedly.
Fixed damage is great when enemies can't heal, and raw damage is the meta.
The fixed damage bomb can bypass the high defense of event bosses.
Pair Jack with heroes who can manipulate the board or finish off low-health enemies to trigger his bomb early.
. Jack plants the bomb. Bjorn uses his double hit to try and kill the bomb carrier. If the target dies, the bomb explodes on the neighbors, creating a chain reaction.
. Treevil softens them up, and Jack finishes them with his AoE passive (since Treevil likely prevented them from buffing, or Jack steals the buffs Treevil missed).
. Any cleanser is Jack's worst nightmare.

Do not pair him with too many other DoT heroes if you are worried about overwrite mechanics, though his bomb is technically a status effect unique enough to usually coexist with standard poisons/burns.

If you pull Jack early, he is a fantastic sniper. The "bomb" might seem confusing, but just treat it as "extra damage later." Level him up, he will serve you well in map stages where bosses have high HP.

Jack is a toy for tournaments. He isn't a "must-level" if you have a roster full of Treevils and Maeves. However, if you enjoy unique mechanics or lack a strong Purple AoE for 3-star restricted modes, he's worth the feeders—but maybe not the limit break aethers unless you just love the Halloween theme.
For years, players have asked, "Why don't the bombs stack?" Users on the forum noted, "I really wish it wouldn't overwrite itself... They should change Jack’s effect to not overwrite."
is more reliable. "I’d honestly rather level a 3rd and 4th Balthazar before him," said one user, citing Jack's squishiness.
Fans of the mechanic compare him to a 3-star Lady Locke: "He is kind of like an even more extreme version of Lady Locke... that bomb is strong!"

Jack is the definition of a "fun" hero. He isn't the most consistent, and his stats are showing their age like an old jack-o'-lantern in November. But when you land that Passive AoE, or when a bomb explodes killing three enemies at once, he provides a satisfaction that boring snipers can't match. He's a trickster with a treat for those patient enough to use him.
Keep one for the collection and niche tournament fun, but don't expect him to carry your competitive roster.
